items.title: From Kubernetes Dashboard to Headlamp: Understanding the Transition
items.summary: <p>For many people, Kubernetes Dashboard was their first window into Kubernetes. It offered a simple visual way to see what was running in a cluster, inspect resources, and build confidence without relying on the command line. For years, it helped developers, students, and operators make sense of
items.reason: Kubernetes UI transition (Dashboard → Headlamp) is a notable change for cluster operators and developers; no existing Wiki page covers this, so a new document is required.

items.title: Reconciling the Past: Correcting Records for Unfixed Kubernetes CVEs
items.summary:
<p>The Kubernetes project relies on transparency to empower cluster administrators and security
researchers. One important way we do that is by publishing CVE records into the Common
Vulnerabilities and Exposures database. As part of our ongoing effort to mature the official
<a href="https://k
items.reason: Discussion of correcting Kubernetes CVE records is directly relevant to security and compliance topics; the Wiki lacks any article on this, so a new entry should be created.

items.title: 5 Software Supply Chain Security Best Practices for Development Teams
items.summary: Understanding software supply chain security is one thing. Putting it into practice across a real pipeline, with real deadlines and real constraints, is another. Most organizations recognize that their software supply chain is a growing attack surface, but translating that awareness into concrete, r
items.reason: Software supply‑chain security best practices are a core concern for modern DevOps; the Wiki currently has no coverage, warranting a dedicated page.

items.title: What is AI Governance? Frameworks, Principles, and Best Practices
items.summary: AI agents are moving fast. According to our State of Agentic AI report, 60% of organizations already have AI agents in production, yet 40% cite security and compliance as the number-one barrier to scaling them further. And that gap between adoption and oversight is exactly where AI governance lives.
items.reason: AI governance frameworks and principles are emerging topics that intersect with security and compliance; a new Wiki article is needed.

items.title: Hardened Images Explained: Fewer CVEs, Smaller Attack Surface
items.summary: When security teams scan their container environments for the first time, they often discover hundreds of known vulnerabilities, and almost none of them trace back to application code. The overwhelming majority come from packages that shipped with the base image: shells, compilers, debug utilities,
items.reason: Explanation of hardened container images and CVE reduction is valuable for security teams; no existing documentation, so a new page should be added.

items.title: What is Software Supply Chain Security?
items.summary: Software supply chain attacks have accelerated faster than most security teams anticipated. Sonatype's 2026 State of the Software Supply Chain report identified more than 454,000 new malicious packages published to open source repositories in 2025, bringing the cumulative total to over 1.2 million s
items.reason: Broad overview of software supply‑chain security aligns with the Wiki’s focus on secure development pipelines; a new article is required.

items.title: How to Secure AI Agents: A Practical Overview for Development Teams
items.summary: In our State of Agentic AI report, 45% of organizations said they struggle to ensure the tools their agents use are secure and enterprise-ready. That number reflects a broader reality: AI agents are moving into production faster than the security practices around them are maturing. The challenge is
items.reason: Securing AI agents is a niche but growing area; the Wiki lacks coverage, so a new document should be created.

items.title: GitHub for Beginners: Answers to some common questions
items.summary:
Find the answers to some of the most common GitHub-related questions.
The post GitHub for Beginners: Answers to some common questions appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: Beginner‑level GitHub Q&A is useful for newcomers and fits the Wiki’s educational scope; a new introductory page is appropriate.

items.title: GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era
items.summary:
GitHub Universe is back: returning to the historic Fort Mason Center in San Francisco on October 28–29, 2026.
The post GitHub Universe is back: All together now, in the agentic era appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: GitHub Universe event summary provides context on community trends; adding a Wiki entry helps track major conferences.

items.title: GitHub Copilot app: The agent-native desktop experience
items.summary:
At Microsoft Build 2026, GitHub introduced new tools, updates, and surfaces so agents can work the way you already work.
The post GitHub Copilot app: The agent-native desktop experience appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: GitHub Copilot desktop experience is a new tool for developers; the Wiki should document its features and usage.

items.title: Still a developer. Just outside. Our latest GitHub Shop collection is here.

The ESC collection lets you escape the confines of your desk and get out into the sun where good ideas are bound to happen.
